# Interview paraphrase (not verbatim)

The subject repeatedly returns to three diagnostic questions when presented with a stuck
decision: "what are you optimizing for," "what would change your mind," and "if the
choice feels binary, the frame is wrong."

These three questions aren't unique to them individually but the sequence — objective
first, falsification second, reframe third — is a recognizable meta-pattern across
multiple conversations.

Source: internal interview notes, paraphrased for confidentiality. Fabricated.
